authorization: Repo Template steady-state manager under the coordinator-accepted custody handback; ordinary bounded Template-local plan and metadata transactions are manager-owned.

plans.priorStatus: draft - blocked on Plan 030 implementation and Code Plan 059 exact landed receipt
plans.rationale: GitHub's public native account defaults are now the sole shared issue-template content authority. Any local .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE file suppresses that source. Template may later own only no-local-override conformance and optional pure validation after the external native-selection canary; it must not publish or mutate issue-template content or labels.

plans.priorStatus: draft - blocked on AO Plan 423 exact landed receipt
plans.rationale: The v1 lineage mixes Template semantics with Registry identity, target paths, model and queue policy, Git and child-process execution, network resolution, destination writes, schedules, enrollment, and activation. Authenticated v1 artifact readback independently confirmed those violations. Preserve the useful deterministic payload, schema, closure, fixture, and release requirements in a breaking pure TypeScript adoption-shell-v2 plan bound to AI-First guide v1.1.0 and ADR-0006.

plans.priorStatus: draft - blocked on exact landed external receipts
plans.canonicalOwner: repo-template release plan plus repo-factory/fleet-registry/queue-canary external owners
plans.rationale: Template owns a generic published release receipt, not GitHub repository creation, target identity, Registry lifecycle, enrollment, schedule reconciliation, capacity, real queue execution, activation, external evidence stores, or issue closure in other repos. A later bounded Template plan will publish only the generic annotated-tag receipt after adoption-shell-v2 and the public issue-template canary; every lifecycle/canary effect remains with its ratified owner.
